Published: May 3, 2026 | Last reviewed: May 3, 2026

Headache Treatment — Causes, Symptoms and Best Treatment in Pakistan

TL;DR — Key Takeaways
  • The most common headache types in Pakistan are tension headache, migraine, and sinus headache
  • Migraine (one-sided headache) worsens with light, sound, and strong smells
  • Paracetamol (Panadol) and ibuprofen (Brufen) are the most common over-the-counter options
  • If you get headaches more than twice a week, see a doctor
  • A sudden severe headache with weakness or difficulty speaking is an emergency — call 1122

What Is a Headache and Why Does It Happen?

A headache is not just one illness — it is a symptom that can have dozens of causes. According to the World Health Organization, 9 out of every 10 adults in Pakistan experience at least one headache per year. The good news is that 90% of headaches are not a sign of serious illness — but it is important to know what type of headache you have so that the right treatment can be given.

Quick Facts: Headaches in Pakistan
Most common typeTension headache (6 out of 10 cases)
Men vs womenMigraine is 3 times more common in women
Children and headachesAlso common in children over 5 years old
When to see a doctor2+ times per week or pain lasting more than 3 hours
Emergency signsSudden severe pain, vomiting, right or left side weakness

4 Common Types of Headache

1. Tension Headache (Most Common)

This type of headache feels as though a tight band is being squeezed around your head. The cause is usually stress, lack of sleep, prolonged screen time on a mobile or laptop, and dehydration. The pain is mild to moderate and can last from 30 minutes to several hours.

2. Migraine (One-Sided Headache)

Migraine is a severe, throbbing pain on one side of the head. Nausea, vomiting, and sensitivity to light and loud sound make the pain worse. In Pakistan, women are more affected, especially between the ages of 20 and 50. A migraine attack can last from 4 hours to 3 days.

3. Sinus Headache

This pain is felt behind the forehead and eyes, especially after a cold or allergy. The pain worsens when you bend forward. Sinus headaches are very common in Pakistan's dusty and smoggy air.

4. Cluster Headache (Rare but Very Severe)

An extremely intense pain behind one eye that recurs daily for weeks. This is quite rare but requires a specialist.

Headache Causes — What Is Triggering Yours?

  • Lack of sleep — getting fewer than 6 hours of sleep
  • Dehydration — the most common trigger in Pakistan's heat
  • Stress and anger — tension from work, home, or relationships
  • Mobile/laptop screen — more than 4 hours of continuous screen time
  • Skipping meals — going on an empty stomach or missing breakfast
  • Caffeine — drinking too much tea or coffee, or stopping suddenly
  • Weather changes — start of rain or summer heat
  • Periods — hormonal headaches in women
  • Scents and perfume — strong fragrances can trigger migraines

Home Remedies for Headache (Try These First)

1. Drink Water — Immediately

The majority of headaches in Pakistan are caused by dehydration. Drink 2 large glasses of water and notice the difference within 30 minutes.

2. Dark Room and Rest

Turn off the lights, set your phone aside, and lie down for 20–30 minutes. This is the most effective approach for migraine.

3. Warm or Cold Compress

For a tension headache, apply a warm towel to your neck. For a migraine, apply a cold ice pack to your forehead. Effects within 10–15 minutes.

4. Ginger Tea

Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that are scientifically proven to help with migraines. Grate one inch of ginger, boil in water, and add honey.

5. Massage

Gently massage the forehead, temples, and neck for 5–10 minutes. Use mustard or coconut oil.

6. Caffeine (Use With Caution)

A small amount of coffee taken at the start of a migraine can reduce the pain — but too much coffee will make the headache worse.

Best Headache Tablets — What to Take?

Over-the-counter (OTC) headache medicines are easily available in Pakistan. The safest options are:

  • Paracetamol (Panadol): 500–1000 mg, every 4–6 hours. No more than 4 grams in 24 hours.
  • Ibuprofen (Brufen 400): 200–400 mg after food. Effective for both migraine and tension headache.
  • Aspirin: 500–1000 mg — never give to children under 16.
  • Migraine-specific: Sumatriptan or rizatriptan — by prescription only.

Has your headache been going on for more than 3 days? Chat with an AI doctor on Ilaaj AI and get a doctor-verified prescription in just a few minutes.

Get Free Advice Now

When to See a Doctor Immediately

Go to emergency care or a doctor right away if you experience:

  • The worst headache of your life — "thunderclap headache"
  • Headache with vomiting, fever, or a stiff neck
  • Weakness on one side of the body, facial drooping, or difficulty speaking (signs of stroke — call 1122 immediately)
  • Headache after a head injury
  • Needing to take medication 3 or more times per week
  • First severe headache after age 50
  • Sudden severe headache in pregnant women (risk of pre-eclampsia)

Headache Prevention — Tips for People in Pakistan

  • Drink 7–8 glasses of water daily, especially in summer
  • Get 7–8 hours of sleep nightly, at a consistent time
  • Use the 20-20-20 rule for screens: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds
  • Do not skip your 3 main meals
  • For stress relief, try prayer, Quran recitation, or 10 minutes of deep breathing
  • Wear a mask when going out on smoggy days
  • Walk for 30 minutes, 3 days per week

Conclusion

Headache treatment depends on the underlying cause. Try home remedies first — water, rest, a dark room, and a mild painkiller. If the headache has lasted more than 3 days or is happening 2 or more times per week, consulting a doctor is essential. On Ilaaj AI, you can describe your symptoms in Urdu or English and receive a doctor-verified prescription in 5 minutes — without waiting in a clinic queue.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the best tablet for headaches in Pakistan?

The safest and most common over-the-counter headache medicine in Pakistan is Paracetamol (Panadol) 500–1000 mg, which can be taken every 4–6 hours. Brufen 400 (ibuprofen) is also effective but should not be taken on an empty stomach. For migraines, specific medicines such as Sumatriptan are available by prescription only. Use Ilaaj AI to consult a doctor.

Why does a one-sided headache (migraine) occur?

Migraine is caused by sudden changes in the blood vessels of the brain. Triggers include hormonal changes (periods in women), lack of sleep, stress, certain foods (cheese, chocolate), strong smells, and weather changes. Migraines are three times more common in women. If they occur 4 or more times per month, consult a neurologist.

What is the immediate treatment for a headache?

Immediately drink 2 large glasses of water, lie down in a dark room, put your phone away, and place a cold ice pack on your forehead. Gently massage your temples. If there is no improvement after 30 minutes, you can take Panadol 500 mg. Ginger tea or a small amount of coffee is often helpful during a migraine attack.

Is getting a headache every day a sign of illness?

Getting a headache every day is not normal. Causes can include chronic tension headache, medication overuse headache, high blood pressure, sinusitis, or occasionally serious conditions such as a brain tumor or intracranial hypertension. If you have a headache 3 or more times per week, see a doctor. You can get immediate advice on Ilaaj AI.

What is the treatment for headache during pregnancy?

The safest headache treatment for pregnant women is rest, hydration, and a cool compress. Paracetamol (Panadol) 500 mg is safe with a doctor's permission, but avoid Brufen and Aspirin during pregnancy. A sudden severe headache combined with blurred vision or swelling of the hands and feet may be a sign of pre-eclampsia — see a doctor immediately.
